Copy, paste, customize.","url":"https://github.com/karanb192/claude-code-hooks"},{"title":"Claude Code hooks explained: the missing layer between prompts and production","url":"https://joseparreogarcia.substack.com/p/claude-code-hooks-explained-the-missing"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks: Automate Your AI Coding Workflow","url":"https://www.ksred.com/claude-code-hooks-a-complete-guide-to-automating-your-ai-coding-workflow/"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks Complete Guide - Deterministic Enforcement Across the Tool Lifecycle | hidekazu-konishi.com","url":"https://hidekazu-konishi.com/entry/claude_code_hooks_complete_guide.html"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks Complete Guide — Automate and Enforce Rules Reliably - SmartScope","url":"https://smartscope.blog/en/generative-ai/claude/claude-code-hooks-guide/"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks: Complete Guide to All 12 Lifecycle Events","url":"https://claudefa.st/blog/tools/hooks/hooks-guide"},{"title":"39+ Claude Code Hooks: Automation Triggers & Scripts","url":"https://www.aitmpl.com/hooks/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what Claude Code hooks are:

## Overview

Hooks are user-defined shell commands, HTTP endpoints, or LLM prompts that execute automatically at specific points in Claude Code's lifecycle.

## Key Characteristics

Hooks inject deterministic processing into Claude Code's lifecycle. Unlike prompt instructions, which are requests that Claude may interpret flexibly, hooks execute the same way every time. This is a crucial distinction—the critical difference from prompt-based rules is that hooks are guaranteed to execute. If you tell Claude Code in your CLAUDE.md not to modify .env files, it will probably listen. If you set up a PreToolUse hook that blocks writes to .env files, it will always block them.

## Handler Types

Command hooks run a shell command. HTTP hooks send the event's JSON input as an HTTP POST request to a URL. MCP tool hooks call a tool on an already-connected MCP server. Prompt hooks send a prompt to a Claude model for single-turn evaluation.

## Common Use Cases

Every time Claude Code writes or edits a file, a PostToolUse hook can run your formatter automatically. No more reviewing diffs full of formatting inconsistencies, no more manually running gofmt or prettier after every change. Other applications include inspecting every bash command before it runs to prevent dangerous operations, and using the Stop event to force Claude to keep working, combined with a prompt hook to verify that all requested tasks were completed.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
